commit.message: Use an assertion failure instead of throwing a new exception

Address PR comments

1. Added an assertion to ensure that `do_peeling()` is never invoked
   when loop peeling is disabled.

2. Avoid bailing out of `create_loop_nest()` when loop peeling is
   disabled.

3. Added a test run that exercises the LoopPeeling=2 case.

Allow finer grain control for disabling loop peeling

Instead of making `LoopPeeling` a boolean flag, it now accepts an
integer in the range 0 through 2 (where 0 implies disable loop peeling
universally, 1 implies enable loop peeling, and 2 implies disable loop
peeling only for iteration splitting).

Address PR comments

1. Use `-Xbatch` only instead of `-Xcomp` and `-Xbatch`, since `-Xcomp`
   implies `-Xbatch` in TestLoopPeeling.java.

2. Use driver mode for the IR test in TestLoopPeelingDisabled.java.

3. Fix style/formatting mistake.

Set debug build as prerequisite for test

The IR framework tests use the `PrintIdeal` and `PrintOptoAssembly`
flags, which are available in debug builds only.

Address portion of PR comments

1. Add a new positive and negative test.  When peeling is enabled, the
   test checks whether the number of counted loops match our
   expectations both before as well as after peeling.  When peeling is
   disabled, the test ensures that the loop peeling compilation phases
   are absent from the IR.

2. Updated `(safepoint != nullptr || !loop->_has_call) && !LoopPeeling`
   to say `!LoopPeeling && (safepoint != nullptr || !loop->_has_call)`
   to make use of short-circuiting.

3. Added a new run to TestLoopPeeling.java to check the correctness of
   the execution in the presence of `-Xbatch`.

4. Restored unnecessary initialization of `safepoint`, since the branch
   condition that follows initializes this variable along both its
   paths.

8371685: C2: Add flag to disable Loop Peeling

This patch adds a new default-true flag called `LoopPeeling` to globally
control whether loop peeling is applied.  In addition to guarding calls
to `do_peeling()`, this patch also adds early bailout checks to ensure
that when loop peeling is disabled, the IR is not left in a partially
modified state.  This is necessary in `create_loop_nest()` for
strip-mined integer loops and after computing a safepoint.

Besides adding a new run configuration in TestLoopPeeling.java, this
patch also adds annotations so that tests that require loop peeling to
be enabled (e.g. vectorization tests) are skipped when loop peeling is
disabled.

Perhaps the `applyIf` annotations that skip tests should be removed,
since loop peeling is enabled by default and if we were to require every
default-enabled flag to be listed among the annotations, then we would
have a prohibitively long list of variables listed in the annotations.
I am happy to update this patch based on the community feedback.
